comment.body:
⚠️📢 **CME Globex Outage - Markets Dark at 3am ET**

Futures went down around 10pm ET last night and they’re still frozen. CME’s saying they’ll let us know when things come back online, but crickets so far on actual timing.

European stocks opened and are trading normally - DAX, FTSE, CAC all flat to slightly up on low holiday volume. They’ve got Eurex for hedging so this is mostly a US problem. Indirect effects from commodity/FX freezes have been minimal so far, but if the outage drags into US cash open, could see some spillover.

**Here’s What I’m Watching For:**

Dude, the real issue is dealers don’t have ES/MES to hedge with right now. They’re gonna have to get creative with their risk management, which means SPX options could price funny once things come back.

If Globex stays dark until cash open, we’re looking at a gap situation where all the hedging that should’ve happened overnight gets compressed into the first few minutes. Short-dated gamma’s gonna reprice fast, dealers will be scrambling without their normal tools, and we could see a quick vol pop as everyone rushes to catch up.

It’s basically like all the flows that would’ve been spread out over 8-10 hours getting jammed into a few minutes. That’s whipsaw city.

The setup’s interesting - when the plumbing breaks and MMs can’t use their usual playbook, stuff gets mispriced. Could be opportunity there if you’re paying attention to the flow when things reopen.

Gotta WATCH the FLOW to be in the KNOW. 🐐

comment.body: I understand speed profile from dealer positioning provides a good foundational understanding of how the customer flow is positioned for the day ( taking the case customer long calls above and short puts below or MM  short risk reversal) - in that case you would position your trade to exploit that area where you expect realized vol to pick up. Or if market has failed to materialize on that expectation, would you rather position to take advantage of selling as dealers have to unwind their hedges as they have deltas for sale before expiration.
comment.raw_body: I understand speed profile from dealer positioning provides a good foundational understanding of how the customer flow is positioned for the day ( taking the case customer long calls above and short puts below or MM  short risk reversal) - in that case you would position your trade to exploit that area where you expect realized vol to pick up. Or if market has failed to materialize on that expectation, would you rather position to take advantage of selling as dealers have to unwind their hedges as they have deltas for sale before expiration.

comment.body:
bcs you can be either net
1) sell OTM puts, buy OTM calls
or
2) buy OTM puts and sell OTM calls

in the first case you short the skew, so market makers will long the skew, (bcs skew is 22d put IV minus 22d call IV per ATM IV) and you generate a negative speed profile.
this means, that the artificial liquidity is pushed downward,
while illiquidity (vacuum) expectations are to the upside.

This pushes the spot/rv correlation upward, meaning you expect a right skewed distribution: lower vol downward, higher vol upward

comment.body:
"Customers are confidently selling volatility in the area of 6750/6700," This can be seen from the OD gamma heatmap; dealer has positive gamma exposure in this area (imagine customers sell puts, confident that the puts expire OTM).

"creating a net negative speed profile, while scaling out of long ITM call positions." Clearly, the speed, which is the rate of change of gamma with respect to spot is negative in 6750/6700 area. Does the gamma "valleys" that appear as time goes by imply customers scale out of long ITM call positions? 

"This area is crucial to hold in terms of continuation of the rally towards 7k into December." See Nov 24 intraday post, if consolidation fails then a further decline to 6220 would happen and it would hardly be bullish.

comment.body:
"Based on the positioning, RV suppression should occur in the 6800/6850 area, with 6850 potentially creating a hard stop for the spot, and 6840 is the strongest local pin. Also note that 6800 becomes a very strong support into the last 1 hour of the session.
If the market would open above 6850, then 6890 is still a hard resistance after 11PM." So at 6800-6840-6850 it looks like a short fly pattern, which tends to pin in the middle. Around 6850 is a big gamma valley, whereas around 6800 a gamma valley appears later, would not be easy to break either.

"If the market would open above 6850, then 6890 is still a hard resistance after 11PM." Similarly, around 6890 a gamma trough appears after 11 am, signifying resistance.

comment.body: "If the market would break below 6800 durin the first 2-3 hours of the session with hightened downside vol, the 6775/50 regio would still be very supportive and potentially push the spot back to 6785." Dealer is net long gamma in this region, so pretty stabilizing, and customers sell volatility in this region, so should be supportive...

comment.body:
"All in all, market is pretty much looks like it wants to hold the pivotal range and aggressively selling volatility here.
Market is pretty much supported. EOM vanna/charm flows are also very supportive for this expo.
So the odds favor for a benign close.
Right now, market is tested if it can build a structure in this marked pivotal area or not. It will decide when the 'C-wave' of the correction comes (in EWT terms)." As volatility is sold, by vanna and charm, an OTM contract's delta decreases and dealer needs to buy back to hedge, which is stabilizing...

comment.body: Just to clarify you can have a negative speed profile - when gamma grows (more positive) below the spot - which essentially means there's a lot of gamma supply below - which makes dealers long the skew & short Vanna. Yes, if there is selling dealers will act as counter-balancing force absorbing the selling flow, resulting in vol compression. But if overall selling exceeds dealer absorption, and implied vol perks up, short vanna will cause dealer to sell deltas - Correct ? Can you please elaborate how doe a positive speed profile (below) ( gamma declines below spot) , and positive speed above ( gamma grows above spot) will effect flow. Or is relative difference between below & upside gamma will determine it
